taxonomy: Pastoraea saviana is classified as a species of sea cucumber in the phylum Echinodermata, which also includes starfish and sea urchins. This classification is based on its unique body structure and the presence of a water vascular system. Pastoraea saviana belongs to the family Gephyrotheridae, a group of deep-sea sea cucumbers. Within the class Holothuroidea, which comprises sea cucumbers, Pastoraea saviana is placed in the order Elasipodida. The species is further identified by its distinctive morphology and distribution in deep-sea environments.
uses: Pastoraea saviana is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties, but its uses in traditional medicine have not been extensively documented. Research on Pastoraea saviana is still in its early stages, and more studies are needed to fully understand its effects on human health.
